incidents.incident_title: Hamas Gaza Assault: Oct 7 2023
incidents.summary: Hamas militants launched a surprise multi-pronged assault from Gaza into southern Israel on Oct 7, 2023, combining mass rocket fire with infantry infiltration via tunnels, motorcycles, and paragliders. Armed fighters overran several Israeli border towns and military posts, resulting in ~1,200 Israeli soldiers and civilians killed and ~240 taken hostage (Israeli figures). The attack included use of drones and anti-tank missiles. The breach of defenses prompted Israel to declare war and initiate a major ground and air offensive in Gaza. The operation revealed significant planning by Hamas and exposed failures in Israeli preparedness.
incidents.assessment: The Oct 7 attack exposed catastrophic failures in Israeli intelligence and border security despite decades of investment. Hamas demonstrated sophisticated multi-domain coordination — rockets, infantry, drones, tunnels — overwhelming the vaunted Gaza Division in hours. The scale of civilian casualties transformed Israeli strategic calculus from deterrence-focused to elimination-focused.
incidents.result_outcome: Hamas gained temporary control of border areas and took hostages; Israel mobilized for major retaliation
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Intelligence failure in surprise attack; Hamas used civilian vehicles and drones; Israel later improved defenses
incidents.osint_sources: H.I. Sutton (analysis of Hamas drones)|https://www.hisutton.com; MAPA_Map (infiltration routes)|https://twitter.com/mapa_map
incidents.press_sources: Reuters (Hamas assault kills 1,200 Oct7 2023)|https://www.reuters.com; AP News (hostage and casualty details)|https://apnews.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: CSIS (Middle East crisis report Oct 2023)|https://www.csis.org; IISS (Eurasia Monthly Nov 2023)|https://www.iiss.org
incidents.tags: GAZA; ISRAEL; HAMAS; ROCKET; TERRORISM; GROUND ASSAULT; CIVILIAN CASUALTIES

incidents.incident_title: IDF Strike: Jabalia Refugee Camp (Nov 2023)
incidents.summary: The Israeli Air Force struck Jabalia refugee camp in Gaza on Nov 1, 2023, targeting a Hamas command center inside multi-story buildings. IAF F-16I jets dropped GBU-39 small diameter bombs onto the sites. Palestinian officials said around 50 people were killed, including a senior Hamas commander. Israel said it was hitting Hamas infrastructure used for rocket launches. The high civilian toll drew condemnation from aid groups. The strike was framed as retaliation for Hamas attacks and a demonstration of Israel's intent to dismantle Hamas leadership.
incidents.assessment: The Jabalia strike exemplified the tension between precision targeting of Hamas leadership in dense urban environments and the resulting civilian toll. Use of SDB munitions in refugee camp settings drew international condemnation and fueled debates about proportionality in urban warfare.
incidents.result_outcome: Hamas infrastructure damaged; significant civilian casualties
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Use of bunker-busting SDB in urban area; entire families reported among dead; first use of this tactic in Gaza war
incidents.press_sources: Reuters (Nov1 2023 strike kills ~50)|https://www.reuters.com; BBC News (strike report)|https://www.bbc.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: ICRC (Gaza conflict summary Nov2023)|https://www.icrc.org; IISS (Middle East security report)|https://www.iiss.org
incidents.tags: GAZA; PALESTINE; ISRAEL; IAF F-16; JABALIA; CIVILIAN CASUALTIES; PRECISION STRIKE; WAR CRIME ALLEGATION

incidents.incident_title: Israeli Airstrike: Iranian Embassy (Damascus)
incidents.summary: Israeli warplanes bombed Iran's diplomatic compound in Damascus on Apr 1, 2024, killing seven Iranian IRGC advisers (including three senior Quds Force commanders). The strike destroyed a consular building adjacent to Iran's embassy. Tehran condemned it as a terrorist act and vowed retaliation. Israel did not comment, but sources later indicated the operation was enabled by Israeli satellite and drone surveillance. This was Israel's first strike on Iran's main diplomatic mission in Damascus, significantly escalating Tehran's war posture.
incidents.personnel_deployed: Unknown number of special forces support
incidents.result_outcome: Iranian consulate building destroyed; 7 IRGC generals killed; Hezbollah condemned and threatened reprisal
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Satellite imagery confirms destruction; Reuters and Iran UN mission statements; unprecedented direct hit on Iran's embassy
incidents.osint_sources: Maxar (after-action satellite imagery)|https://www.maxar.com; H.I. Sutton (analysis of munitions)|https://www.hisutton.com
incidents.press_sources: Reuters (Apr 2 2024)|https://www.reuters.com; BBC (Damascus strike report)|https://www.bbc.com; Times of Israel|https://www.timesofisrael.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: CSIS (Middle East security brief)|https://www.csis.org; IISS (regional conflicts report)|https://www.iiss.org

incidents.incident_title: Iranian Salvo: Drones & Missiles at Israel (True Promise I)
incidents.summary: Iran launched a coordinated barrage of over 200 drones and missiles at Israel on Apr 13-14, 2024, its first direct strike on Israeli territory. The IRGC fired dozens of ground-to-ground missiles (including >10 cruise missiles) and scores of kamikaze UAVs. Israeli air defenses intercepted most threats; only light damage and one injury were reported (a 7-year-old girl). Iran said the attack was retaliation for an Israeli strike on its Damascus consulate. The strike modestly degraded an Israeli military site, prompting vows of significant response by Israel and international calls to contain escalation.
incidents.munitions_weapons: Shahed-191 suicide drones; Zelzal-3 ballistic missiles; cruise missiles
incidents.result_outcome: One Israeli radar array lightly damaged, infrastructure largely intact; 99% of projectiles intercepted
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: 0 — Israeli nuclear and economic infrastructure spared
incidents.intercepted_munitions: Pre-set salvo for G7 time; first IRGC strike inside Israel; US intelligence and cooperation credited
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Maxar (landing debris imagery); Planet Labs (launch pad imagery)
incidents.osint_sources: Reuters (Apr13-14 2024)|https://www.reuters.com; AP News (intercept stats)|https://apnews.com; Jerusalem Post (IDF briefing)|https://www.jpost.com
incidents.press_sources: IISS (missile threat assessment Apr2024)|https://www.iiss.org; CSIS (Iran strike analysis)|https://www.csis.org
incidents.think_tank_sources: IRAN; ISRAEL; MISSILE; DRONE; CRUISE MISSILE; GROUND-TO-GROUND; CIVILIAN INJURY

incidents.incident_title: Ukrainian Assault: Sudzha (Kursk Front)
incidents.summary: Ukrainian forces launched a surprise cross-border offensive into Russia's Kursk region on Aug 7–14 2024, seizing over 1,000 sq km including the town of Sudzha. The raid involved mechanized infantry and artillery, reportedly capturing ~100 Russian POWs. Kyiv said the operation aimed to push the front westward; Russia counterattacked in late 2024 to expel the incursion. This was the first major Ukrainian operation deep in pre-2022 Russian territory, altering battle lines and spurring Moscow to vow retaliation.
incidents.assessment: Ukraine's Kursk incursion was the first significant Ukrainian ground operation inside pre-2022 Russian territory, demonstrating operational surprise capability but ultimately unsustainable without reinforcement. Russia's subsequent recapture underscored the difficulty of holding territory deep in adversary rear areas.
incidents.result_outcome: Ukrainian foothold established; largely retaken by Russia by 2025
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Open-source imagery tracked progress in Sudzha; Zelenskiy hailed the strike; Putin denounced it as provocation
incidents.press_sources: Reuters (Ukraine advances into Kursk Aug14 2024 / Russia retakes Kursk Apr8 2025)|https://www.reuters.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: ISW (Ukraine campaign map Aug 2024)|https://www.understandingwar.org; IISS (Military Balance 2025)|https://www.iiss.org
incidents.tags: UKRAINE; RUSSIA; KURSK; SUDZHA; GROUND ASSAULT; CROSS-BORDER

incidents.incident_title: Hezbollah Rocket Salvo: Haifa Military Complex
incidents.summary: Lebanon's Hezbollah launched rockets at Haifa on Sept 22, 2024, saying it targeted an Israeli military industry complex. The strike was in response to Israeli operations (the pager comms raid) against Hezbollah command nodes. No casualties were reported. Satellite and press analysis suggest a mix of mid-range rockets were fired from southern Lebanon. The attack marked a clear Hezbollah escalation into Israeli urban-industrial areas, increasing pressure on Israel's homeland defense. Israel later denied damage and stressed interception success.
incidents.result_outcome: Confirmed rocket impacts near Haifa arms factory; no reported casualties
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: 0 — no known warship or nuclear site involved
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Hezbollah claimed precision targeting of weapons factory; OSINT imagery shows blast patterns; significant psychological impact in Haifa
incidents.osint_sources: Planet Labs (impact site imagery)|https://www.planet.com; Oryx (munition ID)|https://www.oryxspioenkop.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: IISS (Lebanon conflict tracker Sep2024)|https://www.iiss.org; CSIS (Hezbollah capabilities report)|https://www.csis.org

incidents.incident_title: IDF Special Ops: Southern Lebanon Ground Raids
incidents.summary: In a limited incursion on Oct 1, 2024, Israeli ground forces conducted targeted raids into southern Lebanon. The operations, backed by air force and artillery fire, struck Hezbollah positions in border villages near Israeli communities. No Israeli casualties were reported, and Hezbollah claimed its fighters repelled the attacks. These precision raids aimed to gather intelligence and degrade Hezbollah's frontline capabilities. They marked the first sustained Israeli ground movement across the Blue Line since Hezbollah fired rockets at Israeli border towns, significantly raising the stakes on the northern front.
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: Hezbollah small outposts hit; multiple antennas destroyed (IDF claim)
incidents.economic_impact: 0 — no strategic escalation to nuclear threshold
incidents.osint_sources: Limited ops based on pinpoint intelligence; Israel signaled readiness for larger invasion; US alerts re Lebanon conflict
incidents.press_sources: Maxar (before/after imagery of border strikes)|https://www.maxar.com; @phil_azhari (OSINT analysis)|https://twitter.com/phil_azhari
incidents.tags: Soufan Center (Northern Front briefing Oct2024); IISS (Lebanon security report)

incidents.incident_title: Iranian Ballistic Missiles at Israel (True Promise II)
incidents.summary: Iran launched a volley of ~200 ballistic missiles at Israel on Oct 1, 2024 (early morning), Israel said. The strike, intended as revenge for Israel's Lebanon operations, saw IAF interceptors engage incoming missiles. Israel reported minimal damage and no casualties. The U.S. helped Israel thwart the attack. The barrage significantly escalated hostilities, as it was the largest direct Iranian attack on Israel to date. Israel warned of strong retaliation, marking a critical flashpoint in the Iran–Israel confrontation.
incidents.result_outcome: Several launch barracks and missile depots likely destroyed (Israel claim)
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: 0 — no nuclear or energy sites struck
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Satellite imagery shows impacts in open fields; US intel cited for defense success; triggered White House condemnations
incidents.osint_sources: Maxar (impact sites in Israel)|https://www.maxar.com; Planet Labs (launch signatures)|https://www.planet.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: IISS (Middle East conflict timeline)|https://www.iiss.org; Brookings (analyst commentary)|https://www.brookings.edu

incidents.incident_title: Hezbollah Fadi-1 Missiles: First Direct Hit on Haifa
incidents.summary: Hezbollah fired a barrage of Fadi-1 long-range missiles at northern Israel on Oct 7, 2024, the first direct attack on the city of Haifa. At least two rockets struck Haifa (27 km from Lebanon), causing building damage and wounding civilians, while others hit Tiberias. Israel intercepted several projectiles, but the attack demonstrated Hezbollah's ability to reach Israel's third-largest city. The IDF immediately launched counter-strikes on Hezbollah command infrastructure in Beirut and southern Lebanon. The event marked a new intensity in the cross-border war and prompted international appeals to prevent escalation.
incidents.result_outcome: Damage to Haifa apartment blocks; 10 civilians wounded (roof shrapnel)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: First time Hezbollah's guided rockets penetrated Haifa; video-confirmed impact (Reuters verified); IDF tracking trojan code signals
incidents.osint_sources: Planet Labs (Haifa blast site)|https://www.planet.com; Oryx (missile ID)|https://www.oryxspioenkop.com
incidents.press_sources: Reuters (Oct7 2024)|https://www.reuters.com; IDF press release|https://www.idf.il; Times of Israel|https://www.timesofisrael.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: IISS (MENA analysis Oct2024)|https://www.iiss.org; CSIS (missile warfare report)|https://www.csis.org

incidents.incident_title: Ukraine Storm Shadow Strike: Kursk
incidents.summary: Ukraine fired a salvo of British Storm Shadow cruise missiles into Russia's Kursk region on Nov 20, 2024, striking deep Russian military infrastructure. Russian sources reported at least 14 large explosions near a command post. Kyiv said it targeted rear command centers to disrupt Russian operations. This followed Ukraine's first ATACMS launch a day earlier, signaling an escalation. Russia condemned the use of Western long-range missiles as a major escalation and reportedly raised its nuclear alert level.
incidents.assessment: The Storm Shadow strike marked the first combat use of UK-supplied cruise missiles against targets inside Russia. The escalation triggered Russian nuclear posture adjustments and set a precedent for Western long-range weapons employment that shaped subsequent US policy decisions.
incidents.result_outcome: Russian command post reportedly damaged; no confirmed casualties
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: Nuclear posture raised; strategic pressure on Russia increased
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: First combat use of UK Storm Shadow in Russian territory
incidents.osint_sources: Two Majors (Telegram missile debris)|https://t.me/twomajors; Planet Labs (impact imagery)|https://www.planet.com
incidents.press_sources: Reuters (Storm Shadow strikes Kursk Nov 21 2024)|https://www.reuters.com; Al Jazeera (analysis)|https://www.aljazeera.com
incidents.tags: UKRAINE; RUSSIA; STORM SHADOW; CRUISE MISSILE; KURSK; ESCALATION

incidents.incident_title: Israeli Air Offensive: Strikes on Iran (Operation Inherent Resolve)
incidents.summary: Over Oct 25-26, 2024 Israel launched a major airstrike campaign deep into Iran. Strikes hit IRGC facilities near Tehran, Parchin missile complex, and sites in western Iran (Ilam, Khuzestan) with bunker-penetrating munitions. Iran reported limited damage and said it shot down or deterred the jets. The raids were explicitly retaliation for Iran's Oct 1 missile assault. This was the first massive Israeli aerial strike on Iran itself, signaling a severe escalation. Iran blamed Israeli light warheads and claimed minimal impact. Analysts noted damage to Parchin missile labs confirming hits.
incidents.result_outcome: Multiple IRGC missile production buildings destroyed; Iranian air defences engaged with little success
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: New precedent of Israel striking Iranian homeland; precise targeting (Kharg island, Parchin) noted; U.S. liaison confirmed attack
incidents.osint_sources: Planet Labs (damage at Parchin)|https://www.planet.com; H.I. Sutton (munitions analysis)|https://www.hisutton.com
incidents.think_tank_sources: IISS (Iran nuclear issue report 2024)|https://www.iiss.org; CSIS (Israeli strike analysis)|https://www.csis.org

incidents.incident_title: GBU-57 MOP Strike: Fordow Nuclear Facility
incidents.summary: Seven B-2 Spirit stealth bombers flew round-trip from Diego Garcia carrying GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators — the only weapon capable of penetrating Fordow's deep underground bunker buried ~80–90m inside a mountain near Qom. 52 tanker aircraft required for aerial refueling support. Twelve MOPs required vs. two at Natanz reflecting extreme hardening. Iran pre-evacuated 440.9 kg enriched uranium including 408.9 kg at 60%-enriched (~9 weapons worth).
incidents.assessment: Fordow was specifically designed to survive conventional bunker-busters. The GBU-57 MOP was developed precisely for this mission. The pre-strike evacuation of uranium indicates Iran had advance warning or a prepared contingency. NBC News later assessed only Fordow was definitively destroyed of the three primary nuclear targets.
incidents.result_outcome: Facility destroyed
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: 440.9 kg uranium evacuated pre-strike incl. 408.9 kg 60%-enriched (~9 weapons worth); 52 tanker aircraft required; first MOP combat use; NBC News assessed only Fordow definitively destroyed
incidents.osint_sources: Oliver Alexander (OSINT) — F-22 deployment 4-day warning|https://twitter.com/OAlexanderDK; MATA — 100+ tankers 200+ cargo tracked|https://twitter.com/MT_Anderson
incidents.press_sources: The War Zone|https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone; Air & Space Forces Magazine|https://www.airandspaceforces.com; CNN (satellite imagery)|https://www.cnn.com
incidents.tags: MOP FIRST USE; NUCLEAR STRIKE; B-2 SPIRIT; GBU-57; DIEGO GARCIA

incidents.incident_title: GBU-57 Strike: Natanz Enrichment Complex
incidents.summary: Secondary MOP strike on Natanz. Two MOPs deployed vs. 12 at Fordow reflecting shallower burial depth (~8m). Natanz houses thousands of IR-1 IR-4 IR-6 centrifuges. NBC News assessed destruction as partial and effectiveness disputed.
incidents.assessment: NBC News found that of the three primary targets, only Fordow was definitively destroyed. Two MOPs vs. twelve at Fordow suggests planners underestimated the hardened underground halls.
incidents.result_outcome: Partial destruction — effectiveness disputed
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Natanz under IAEA safeguards with cameras and monitoring
incidents.press_sources: NBC News (assessment: only Fordow destroyed)|https://www.nbcnews.com; The War Zone|https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone
incidents.think_tank_sources: Arms Control Association|https://www.armscontrol.org; Arms Control Wonk|https://www.armscontrolwonk.com
incidents.tags: NATANZ; CENTRIFUGE HALLS; B-2 SPIRIT; PARTIAL EFFECT

incidents.incident_title: Tomahawk Barrage: Isfahan Nuclear Complex
incidents.origin_label: US Navy Persian Gulf / Gulf of Oman
incidents.summary: Tomahawk TLAM barrage (24+) on Isfahan nuclear research facilities including uranium conversion and fuel fabrication. IAEA later found Iran had secretly stored enriched uranium under the Isfahan site — a hidden cache not declared to inspectors before the strikes.
incidents.assessment: The hidden uranium discovery raises questions about the completeness of Iran's declaration. Pre-strike IAEA assessment did not account for this cache.
incidents.result_outcome: Partial destruction
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: IAEA found hidden enriched uranium under Isfahan (Haaretz Feb 27 2026); hidden cache not declared to inspectors

incidents.incident_title: IDF 12-Day Air Campaign: Iran
incidents.target_sublabel: IRGC, C2, TEL sites, air defense
incidents.summary: Israel's most expansive offensive air campaign since 1973. 1400+ sorties over 12 days. F-35I Adir as primary platform for stealth penetration of Iranian air defenses. Opening salvo simultaneously eliminated 40 senior IRGC commanders. 120 Iranian TELs destroyed eliminating primary ballistic missile delivery capacity.
incidents.assessment: The F-35I's low-observable design allowed penetration of Iranian S-300 defenses. Destroying 120 TELs in opening phases effectively eliminated Iran's near-term retaliatory ballistic missile capability. Simultaneous decapitation of 40 commanders unprecedented in modern warfare.
incidents.platform_or_unit: F-35I Adir / F-15I Ra'am / F-16I Sufa
incidents.target_type: IRGC C2, TEL sites, air defense, leadership

incidents.incident_title: PLA Island Upgrades: Mischief Reef Expansion
incidents.summary: CSIS AMTI satellite monitoring confirmed continued expansion and hardening of PLA installations at Mischief Reef in 2025. Additional aircraft hangar construction; upgrade of missile launcher positions; deployment of additional coastal defense cruise missile (CDCM) batteries. Mischief Reef sits within Philippine EEZ 250km from Palawan. 2016 PCA tribunal ruled PLA occupation unlawful.
incidents.assessment: Mischief Reef's strategic position in the Philippine EEZ, combined with its hardened military infrastructure, creates a persistent forward-deployed threat to the Philippines that effectively negates Philippine sovereign rights over the feature and its surrounding waters. The missile systems deployed can threaten Philippine and US naval vessels throughout the western Philippines.
incidents.platform_or_unit: PLAN construction vessels + Southern Theater Command garrison
incidents.munitions_weapons: YJ-12B CDCM (400km range ASCM); HQ-9B SAM system; garrison weapons
incidents.target_type: Artificial island military base — Philippine EEZ
incidents.result_outcome: Expanded military infrastructure — CDCM and SAM deployed
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: 3000m runway capable of fighter/bomber operations; YJ-12B can threaten PHL and US naval vessels throughout western Philippines
incidents.tags: MISCHIEF REEF; CDCM DEPLOYED; ILLEGAL OCCUPATION; PHL EEZ; YJ-12B

incidents.incident_title: PLAN Carrier Strike Group: Spratlys Show-of-Force
incidents.origin_label: Hainan Island — PLAN Yulin Naval Base
incidents.summary: PLA Navy Shandong carrier strike group conducted extended operations in and around the Spratly Islands centered on Fiery Cross Reef. Demonstrated China's increasing ability to project naval power throughout the contested SCS and pressure Philippine and Vietnamese claimants simultaneously. J-15 carrier aircraft conducted air defense exercises over disputed maritime zones.
incidents.assessment: The Shandong CSG operation combined with the hardened Fiery Cross base creates a layered anti-access architecture that significantly complicates any US/Philippine military response. The J-15 exercises over the Spratlys asserted Chinese air dominance over disputed waters.
incidents.platform_or_unit: CV-17 Shandong (Type 001A) + 2× Type 055 + 4× Type 052D + 1× Type 093 SSN
incidents.munitions_weapons: J-15B carrier fighters + Z-9 ASW helicopters + ship-borne weapons systems
incidents.result_outcome: ~14 days extended operations; J-15 exercises over Spratlys; PHL BRP Jose Rizal + P-3C deployed in response
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Fiery Cross Reef: 3000m runway; hardened aircraft shelters; SAM systems; CDCM batteries; Shandong CSG combined with Fiery Cross base creates layered A2/AD architecture
incidents.tags: SHANDONG CSG; SPRATLYS; SHOW OF FORCE; FIERY CROSS

incidents.incident_title: CCG Mass Blockade: Scarborough Shoal (Philippines)
incidents.summary: China Coast Guard deployed up to 40 vessels simultaneously to enforce de-facto blockade of Scarborough Shoal. CCG vessels used water cannons, sound devices, and laser blinding against Philippine Coast Guard ships. Prevented Filipino fishermen from accessing traditional fishing grounds inside Philippine EEZ. 2016 PCA tribunal ruled China has no legal basis for claims.
incidents.assessment: Scarborough Shoal blockade represents China's "salami slicing" gray-zone strategy — incremental coercion below the threshold of armed conflict. Each iteration of harassment normalizes the next level of coercion. The US-Philippines Mutual Defense Treaty (MDT) technically covers Philippine armed forces but has been deliberately not invoked for CCG-on-PCG harassment.
incidents.platform_or_unit: China Coast Guard (CCG) — up to 40 vessels
incidents.munitions_weapons: Water cannons; sound devices; laser dazzlers; physical blockade
incidents.result_outcome: CCG blockade sustained; Filipino fishermen excluded; FONOPS conducted by USN
incidents.economic_impact: Brent crude minimal impact; Philippine fisheries economic damage
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: 2016 PCA tribunal: China has NO legal basis for claims; blockade represents salami-slicing gray-zone strategy below armed conflict threshold; US-PHL MDT technically covers armed forces but not CCG-on-PCG harassment
incidents.tags: 40 CCG VESSELS; WATER CANNON; SCARBOROUGH SHOAL; GRAY-ZONE

incidents.incident_title: Second Thomas Shoal: Resupply Interdiction Escalates
incidents.target_sublabel: BRP Sierra Madre — PHL military outpost
incidents.summary: CCG and PAFMM conducted systematic interdiction of Philippine military resupply missions to BRP Sierra Madre — a deliberately grounded WWII-era ship serving as PHL military outpost. CCG used water cannons, sound devices, and in at least one incident directed-energy weapons. Philippine sailors injured. Shoal sits within Philippine EEZ and was ruled PHL sovereign territory by 2016 PCA tribunal.
incidents.assessment: Second Thomas Shoal has become the most kinetically active gray-zone flashpoint in Asia. China's willingness to use directed-energy weapons against Philippine sailors marks an escalation from prior water-cannon-only harassment. The BRP Sierra Madre is deliberately kept in deteriorating condition by the Philippines to maintain a permanent physical presence — replacing it would require a Chinese decision to allow or physically block the operation.
incidents.platform_or_unit: China Coast Guard (CCG) + PAFMM militia fishing vessels (100+)
incidents.munitions_weapons: Water cannons; sound devices; directed-energy weapon (DEW); physical ramming
incidents.target_type: PHL military outpost — BRP Sierra Madre
incidents.result_outcome: Ongoing interdiction — resupply missions harassed; BRP Sierra Madre deteriorating
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Second Thomas most kinetically active gray-zone flashpoint in Asia; DEW use marks escalation from water-cannon-only harassment; BRP Sierra Madre deliberately kept deteriorating to maintain physical presence
incidents.tags: DEW ATTACK; BRP SIERRA MADRE; PAFMM; PHL EEZ; SECOND THOMAS

incidents.incident_title: USS [DDG] FONOP: China Intercepts with J-16 Fighter
incidents.summary: US Navy Arleigh Burke DDG conducted FONOP within 12nm of PLA-constructed artificial island asserting China's straight baselines are unlawful under UNCLOS. PLAAF J-16 Flanker-D fighters intercepted the destroyer conducting unsafe maneuvers at close distance. PLAAF has conducted 180+ unsafe intercepts of US and allied aircraft since 2021.
incidents.assessment: The J-16 intercept was one of ~180+ unsafe intercepts the PLA has conducted since 2021. Each FONOP is legally necessary to prevent China's claims from becoming accepted by acquiescence. The PLA's response escalation from protests to physical intercepts represents a concerning trend toward possible miscalculation.

incidents.result_outcome: FONOP completed; UNCLOS rights asserted

incidents.incident_title: PLAAF: 103-Aircraft Incursion — Taiwan ADIZ
incidents.summary: PLAAF and PLAN aviation conducted record 103-aircraft incursion into Taiwan's ADIZ over 24 hours including H-6K nuclear-capable bombers, J-20 stealth fighters, J-16 strike fighters, and Y-8 ELINT. ROC Air Force scrambled 66 fighters. Timed to US preoccupation with Iran/Venezuela operations — deliberate signal probing US capacity for simultaneous contingency management.
incidents.assessment: The 103-aircraft incursion exceeded the prior record of 56 aircraft. Timing during US-Iran/Venezuela operations is a deliberate signal that China is probing US capacity to simultaneously manage a Taiwan contingency alongside active Middle East and Caribbean operations. H-6K inclusion is a direct nuclear signaling component.
incidents.platform_or_unit: PLAAF + PLAN aviation — H-6K bomber; J-20 stealth; J-16; Y-8 ELINT
incidents.munitions_weapons: Nuclear-capable H-6K bombs + conventional PGMs + ship-borne weapons
incidents.result_outcome: Record ADIZ incursion; ROC scrambled 66 fighters; USS Ronald Reagan repositioned to Philippine Sea
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Timing during US-Iran/Venezuela operations is deliberate signal probing US capacity for simultaneous Taiwan contingency; H-6K inclusion is direct nuclear signaling; exceeds prior record of 56 aircraft
incidents.tags: 103 AIRCRAFT; H-6K BOMBER; TAIWAN ADIZ; NUCLEAR SIGNAL; RECORD INCURSION

incidents.incident_title: MV Ocean Trader: SOF Mothership Staging
incidents.origin_label: MV Ocean Trader converted civilian vessel
incidents.summary: MV Ocean Trader — converted civilian cargo vessel serving as classified SOF maritime staging platform — tracked via ESA Sentinel-2 commercial satellite imagery by OSINT analysts. USS Lake Erie (Ticonderoga-class CG-70) escorted vessel ~200nm off Venezuelan coast. SOF helicopters later observed departing for the Caracas raid.
incidents.assessment: The Sentinel-2 detection demonstrates that free commercial 10m-resolution satellite imagery combined with AIS gap analysis can expose classified SOF mothership operations. Operational security for future similar missions must account for ubiquitous commercial satellite coverage.
incidents.platform_or_unit: MV Ocean Trader (SOF mothership); USS Lake Erie (CG-70)
incidents.result_outcome: SOF forward staging established
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Detected by ESA Sentinel-2 (free commercial satellite); @MT_Anderson and The Iceman (Substack) identified vessel publicly before operation commenced; SOF helicopters observed flying over Caracas during raid
incidents.tags: SOF MOTHERSHIP; OSINT TRACKED; SENTINEL-2; USS LAKE ERIE

incidents.incident_title: IDF Pre-War Hezbollah Degradation: Nov 2024–Feb 2026 Ceasefire Strikes
incidents.target_label: Southern Lebanon + Beqaa Valley + North of Litani
incidents.target_sublabel: Hezbollah missile array, Radwan Unit, engineering infrastructure
incidents.summary: During the November 2024–February 2026 ceasefire period, the IDF continued targeted strikes against Hezbollah across Lebanon on a near-daily basis, citing Hezbollah's failure to disarm and withdraw per the ceasefire terms. By the time Hezbollah entered the war in March 2026, the IDF had: eliminated 267+ Hezbollah operatives (confirmed via open source); killed 20 operatives in February 2026 alone (44 total strikes that month, a 50% decrease from January's 88 strikes); destroyed engineering vehicles used to rehabilitate terror infrastructure; targeted weapons depots in civilian areas (with advance warnings); struck rocket launchers and Radwan Unit camps; and maintained 5 border positions in Lebanese territory. February 2026 strikes included killing 8 Hezbollah missile force operatives in Beqaa Valley, including a 'commander' — one of the deadliest ceasefire-period strikes. Strike geography: south of Litani, north of Litani (increasing), and Beqaa Valley (consistent presence).
incidents.assessment: The IDF's continuous ceasefire-period degradation represents a sustained attrition campaign that significantly weakened Hezbollah before it re-entered open hostilities. Eliminating 267 operatives over 15 months — including missile array commanders, Radwan Unit officers, air defense personnel, and engineering department heads — systematically degraded Hezbollah's reconstitution effort. This established the conditions for the IDF's rapid offensive campaign response when Hezbollah fired in March 2026. The pattern mirrors IDF doctrine in Gaza: maintain constant pressure even under ceasefire conditions to prevent adversary reconstitution.
incidents.munitions_quantity: 267+ operatives KIA across campaign; 44 strikes in Feb 2026 alone
incidents.target_type: Hezbollah missile array / Radwan Unit / engineering department / air defense / sector commanders
incidents.result_outcome: 267+ Hezbollah operatives eliminated; Hezbollah missile and engineering arrays significantly degraded; Radwan Unit leadership attrited
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Alma Research Center tracks confirmed eliminations via open source; IRGC officers personally briefing Hezbollah missile unit in Beqaa Valley (Times of Israel, pre-war); Hezbollah refused to disarm north of Litani throughout ceasefire; Israel held 5 border positions citing security concerns
incidents.osint_sources: Alma Research Center (monthly strike tracker — most comprehensive OSINT source for this campaign)
incidents.tags: 267 OPERATIVES|CEASEFIRE STRIKES|RADWAN UNIT|MISSILE ARRAY|CONTINUOUS ATTRITION|BEQAA VALLEY

incidents.incident_title: LUCAS Ship-Launch Test: USS Santa Barbara (LCS)
incidents.origin_sublabel: CENTCOM AOR — first ship-based LUCAS test
incidents.target_sublabel: Two weeks after TFSS announced; first naval LUCAS fire
incidents.summary: Two weeks after CENTCOM announced Task Force Scorpion Strike, US Navy personnel test-fired a LUCAS drone from the Independence-class Littoral Combat Ship USS Santa Barbara (LCS-32) in the Persian Gulf — the first ship-based launch of the system. CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper posted 'Bravo Zulu' on social media confirming the test. The launch demonstrated the potential for LCS vessels to serve as LUCAS motherships, extending LUCAS's reach into the maritime domain and setting conditions for sea-based one-way attack drone operations alongside Task Force 59 (maritime drone task force).
incidents.assessment: The combination of Task Force Scorpion Strike (land-based LUCAS) and Task Force 59 (sea-based autonomous systems) indicates CENTCOM is building an integrated, multi-domain drone strike network across the region. LCS vessels, previously criticized for limited combat utility, may find a new role as LUCAS launch platforms. Ship-based LUCAS deployment would enable strikes from sea approaches not covered by ground basing — significantly complicating Iranian targeting of launch sites.
incidents.platform_or_unit: Task Force Scorpion Strike + US Navy (CENTCOM AOR)
incidents.result_outcome: Test successful
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed via CENTCOM social media; linked to broader CENTCOM drone network with Task Force 59
incidents.tags: LUCAS|LCS|NAVAL DRONE|TASK FORCE 59|FIRST SHIP LAUNCH|PRE-COMBAT PROOF

incidents.incident_title: Op. Roaring Lion: Khamenei Eliminated
incidents.summary: CIA and Mossad joint operation to eliminate Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei. CIA tracked him for months. He hid in underground bunker; officials meeting him were required to do so blindfolded to prevent disclosure of location. CIA penetration of security apparatus enabled precise location. Killed by precision airstrike Feb 28 2026. Triggered Iranian regime crisis and emergency UNSC session.
incidents.assessment: Khamenei's killing — Supreme Leader since 1989 — creates a succession crisis with no clear mechanism. CIA penetration of his security apparatus despite blindfolded-official protocols represents one of the most significant intelligence coups since Bin Laden.
incidents.result_outcome: Khamenei KILLED — Iranian regime crisis triggered
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Iranian regime crisis post-kill; succession crisis — Guardian Council must select new Supreme Leader; Emergency UNSC session; officials met Khamenei blindfolded to prevent location disclosure

incidents.incident_title: Opening Salvo: 40 Senior IRGC Commanders Killed
incidents.summary: 40 senior IRGC commanders eliminated simultaneously in opening salvo of Epic Fury / Roaring Lion. Largest single military leadership decapitation in modern warfare history. Simultaneous execution prevented warning spreading between targets.

incidents.incident_title: LUCAS Combat Debut: First US One-Way Attack Drones vs. Iran
incidents.origin_label: US Army / CENTCOM AORR (Kuwait / Middle East)
incidents.origin_sublabel: Task Force Scorpion Strike — under SOCCENT
incidents.target_label: Iran — IRGC Command Nodes + Air Defense + Missile Sites
incidents.target_sublabel: Multiple fixed military targets across western and central Iran
incidents.summary: CENTCOM's Task Force Scorpion Strike (TFSS) deployed the Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System (LUCAS) in combat for the first time in history during Operation Epic Fury. Formed in December 2025 under SOCCENT after SecDef Hegseth directed 'Unleashing US Military Drone Dominance' in July 2025, TFSS employed ground-launched LUCAS drones against IRGC command and control nodes, air defense assets, missile launch infrastructure, and military airfields. The LUCAS is a reverse-engineered Shahed-136 built by Arizona-based SpektreWorks — a V-shaped delta-wing loitering munition powered by a DA-215 two-cylinder engine, ~10ft long, 8ft wingspan, ~$35,000 per unit. It features autonomous coordination, Starlink terminals enabling swarm networking, and can be launched via catapult, RATO, or vehicle-based systems. A LUCAS was first ship-launched from USS Santa Barbara (LCS) in December 2025. CENTCOM announced: 'These low-cost drones, modeled after Iran's Shahed drones, are now delivering American-made retribution.' A malfunction video of an early LUCAS launch circulated on social media but was unconfirmed by CENTCOM.
incidents.assessment: LUCAS is the US answer to the drone cost-exchange asymmetry that has favored Iran and Russia throughout the 2020s. At $35,000 per unit versus $4M+ for a Patriot PAC-3 interceptor, the US is now fielding mass-scale expendable munitions at cost parity with adversary systems. The deliberate irony of using reverse-engineered Iranian drones against Iran was intentional — a symbolic and strategic message. LUCAS's Starlink-enabled swarm coordination represents a step-change beyond the simple fixed-target Shahed, enabling dynamic targeting and autonomous cooperative strikes. The combat debut 8 months after public unveiling is a compression of the normal US acquisition timeline, signaling a new doctrine of rapid fielding under wartime conditions.
incidents.munitions_weapons: LUCAS (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System) — SpektreWorks FLM-136 derivative
incidents.range_km: ~800 km (estimated, based on Shahed-136 baseline ~2000 km range)
incidents.target_type: IRGC C2 / air defense / missile infrastructure / military airfields
incidents.result_outcome: First combat use confirmed — targets struck, BDA classified
incidents.economic_impact: Part of Brent +9% spike; broader Epic Fury economic impact
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reverse-engineered from captured Iranian Shahed-136; MUSIC mesh network enables swarm ops; Starlink terminals for dynamic targeting; Dec 2025 ship-launch test from USS Santa Barbara (LCS)
incidents.osint_sources: The War Zone (TFSS creation Dec 2025); DefenseScoop (LUCAS remains ready Mar 2026)
incidents.press_sources: Military Times; Fox News; Task & Purpose; Reuters; Defense Scoop
incidents.tags: LUCAS FIRST USE|DRONE SWARM|SHAHED CLONE|TASK FORCE SCORPION|SOCCENT|$35K DRONE

incidents.summary: Tomahawk saturation from 13 Arleigh Burke DDGs. Each carries up to 96 TLAMs (theoretical max ~1248 total). Largest TLAM volley since Operation Iraqi Freedom 2003. Targets: IRGC Quds Force bases; missile production; air defense; C2 nodes.
incidents.assessment: Sheer volume of Tomahawk fire created a saturation challenge for Iranian air defenses, which had already been significantly degraded by the IDF 12-day campaign.
incidents.target_type: C2, missile production, air defense, IRGCN
incidents.result_outcome: Iranian military infrastructure degraded
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Each Arleigh Burke carries up to 96 Tomahawks in VLS; 13 ships = ~1248 theoretical capacity; largest TLAM volley since OIF 2003
incidents.press_sources: Military Times|https://www.militarytimes.com; The War Zone|https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone; Gulf News|https://gulfnews.com
incidents.tags: 13 DESTROYERS; TOMAHAWK; VLS SATURATION; LARGEST SINCE 2003

incidents.incident_title: Minab Strike — Worst Single Incident (148 Killed)
incidents.summary: Strike on Minab resulted in 148 deaths — single deadliest incident of the entire campaign. Total Iranian civilian casualties: 555+ (Iranian Red Crescent). Cited at emergency UN Security Council meeting. Attribution contested — US/Israel deny deliberate civilian targeting.
incidents.assessment: Whether the strike resulted from GPS drift, intelligence failure, or deliberate targeting remains disputed. The incident became the focal point for international condemnation and underscores the challenge of precision strikes in densely populated coastal areas.
incidents.result_outcome: 148 civilians killed; worst single incident
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Attribution contested — US/Israel deny deliberate civilian targeting; GPS drift or intelligence failure possible
incidents.press_sources: Air & Space Forces Magazine|https://www.airandspaceforces.com; UNSC Security Council Report|https://www.un.org/securitycouncil
incidents.tags: 148 KILLED; CIVILIAN STRIKE; 555+ TOTAL CIVN; DISPUTED

incidents.incident_title: Strike: Southern Forward Naval Headquarters (Bandar Abbas)
incidents.origin_sublabel: Platforms not specified in public reporting
incidents.target_sublabel: Southern Forward Naval Headquarters (Artesh Navy) / naval HQ complex (reported)
incidents.summary: CTP-ISW assessed that U.S. and Israeli strikes hit Iran’s Southern Forward Naval Headquarters in Bandar Abbas on March 1; Reuters also reported Trump said strikes largely destroyed Iran’s naval headquarters while claiming multiple Iranian warships destroyed.
incidents.assessment: Naval C2 disruption: this HQ reportedly coordinates the Artesh Navy Southern Fleet and operations across multiple naval regions; attribution and exact strike package not disclosed publicly.
incidents.result_outcome: Naval HQ hit / degraded (reported)
incidents.economic_impact: Reuters reported large-scale shipping risk escalation; Hormuz traffic disruptions and rerouting intensified after the opening days of strikes.
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Trump publicly claimed nine Iranian warships destroyed and naval HQ pummeled; CTP-ISW linked the HQ claim to the Southern Forward Naval HQ strike.

incidents.incident_title: Chabahar Pier Strike: Jamaran-class/Moudge Warship Reported Sunk
incidents.origin_sublabel: Reported at/near Chabahar pier (Gulf of Oman)
incidents.summary: Reuters reported CENTCOM said it sank an Iranian Jamaran-class corvette in the Gulf of Oman at a Chah Bahar pier; CTP-ISW also assessed the U.S. sank the IRGC Navy Jamaran (Moudge-class) near the Imam Ali Base in Chabahar.
incidents.assessment: High-confidence vessel loss claim from CENTCOM statement; public reporting still sparse on munition type (missile/air-delivered) and exact strike geometry.
incidents.result_outcome: Warship reported sunk (CENTCOM)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: CTP-ISW notes Jamaran’s prior history (incl. seizure of U.S. unmanned surface vessels in Sept 2022) and role in regional maritime pressure campaigns.

incidents.incident_title: B-2 Strikes: Iranian Ballistic Missile Facilities
incidents.origin_label: Unknown (U.S. launch locations not specified in public reporting)
incidents.target_label: Iranian ballistic missile facilities (locations not specified)
incidents.summary: U.S. reporting described B-2 stealth bombers striking Iranian ballistic missile facilities with 2,000-lb bombs during Operation Epic Fury.
incidents.assessment: Confirmed U.S. use of B-2s and 2,000-lb-class munitions against missile infrastructure; specific sites, weapons variants, and bomb counts not publicly specified in the cited reporting.
incidents.platform_or_unit: U.S. Air Force B-2 stealth bombers
incidents.result_outcome: Facilities struck; damage extent undisclosed
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: Indirect (targets were missile facilities, not nuclear sites)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters reported B-2s dropped 2,000-lb bombs on hardened, underground missile facilities; AP reported B-2s hit ballistic missile facilities with 2,000-lb bombs.
incidents.osint_sources: CENTCOM Operation Epic Fury fact sheet (first 72 hours)|https://www.centcom.mil/Portals/6/Documents/Publications/260303-Fact%20Sheet.pdf

incidents.incident_title: Tehran Air Raids: Air Force, Missile Command & Internal Security Buildings
incidents.target_sublabel: Approx. city centroid; specific aimpoints not publicly detailed
incidents.summary: AP reported multiple waves of Israeli fighter-jet strikes in Tehran hitting buildings tied to Iran’s air force, missile command, and an internal security force.
incidents.assessment: Target categories are described by AP; independent public geolocation of exact buildings/coordinates is limited in the cited sources.
incidents.result_outcome: Buildings struck; damage extent undisclosed
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: CENTCOM press release describes partner strikes against IRGC C2, air defense, missile/drone launch sites, and military airfields (broad target categories consistent with AP-described strikes).

incidents.incident_title: Tehran Strike Damages Gandhi Hospital Near State TV Communications Tower
incidents.target_sublabel: Gandhi Hospital / state TV communications tower area (exact coordinates not specified in sources)
incidents.summary: Reporting described a strike in Tehran that hit near a state TV communications tower and nearby buildings, damaging Gandhi Hospital.
incidents.assessment: Hospital damage is corroborated by AP photo-caption reporting and a Reuters photo description; attribution and exact aimpoint remain unclear in the cited sources.
incidents.result_outcome: Communications-tower area struck; hospital damaged
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters photo description references a damaged state TV communications tower and destroyed building next to Gandhi Hospital; AP photo-caption notes hospital damage after a strike hit a state TV communications tower and nearby buildings.

incidents.incident_title: Strike Destroys Residence Complex Belonging to Supreme Leader Khamenei
incidents.target_sublabel: Destroyed residence complex (per satellite imagery caption)
incidents.summary: Reuters satellite imagery captions described a destroyed residence complex belonging to Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on March 1.
incidents.assessment: Damage is supported by satellite imagery captions; operational details (platform/munitions) and exact sub-location not provided in cited sources.
incidents.result_outcome: Residence complex destroyed (reported by satellite imagery captions)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: AP described a coordinated mission against Iranian leadership as a 'golden opportunity'; Reuters imagery captions document destruction of a Khamenei residence complex in Tehran.

incidents.incident_title: Konarak Naval Base: Naval Vessels and Buildings Destroyed
incidents.summary: Reuters satellite imagery captions described destroyed naval vessels and buildings at Konarak after U.S. and Israeli strikes on March 1.
incidents.assessment: Satellite captions indicate significant damage; public reporting does not specify the precise ships destroyed, weapon types, or sortie counts for this specific Konarak strike.
incidents.platform_or_unit: Unknown aircraft / missiles (U.S. and Israeli strikes reported)
incidents.result_outcome: Naval vessels and base buildings destroyed (reported)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: CENTCOM fact sheet lists Iranian Navy ships/submarines among target types; AP reported U.S./Israel aimed at warships and said multiple Iranian warships were sunk.

incidents.incident_title: Zahedan Airbase: Radar System Destroyed
incidents.target_sublabel: Radar system destroyed (per satellite imagery caption)
incidents.summary: Reuters satellite imagery captions described a destroyed radar system at Zahedan airbase on March 1.
incidents.assessment: Destruction of a radar system suggests degradation of air defense / early warning; no public details on the strike package or follow-on effects in cited sources.
incidents.result_outcome: Radar destroyed (reported)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: CENTCOM press release and fact sheet list Iranian air defense capabilities / integrated air defense systems among target types.

incidents.incident_title: Minab: Elementary School Strike Mass Casualties (Reported)
incidents.summary: AP reported Iran’s foreign minister shared an aerial photo and said more than 160 girls were killed in a U.S.-Israeli strike on an elementary school in Minab; Reuters later reported Iran said 165 girls were killed when their school was bombed on the war’s first day.
incidents.assessment: Casualty figures and target description rely on Iranian official/media claims cited by AP/Reuters; independent verification is not included in the cited sources here.
incidents.result_outcome: School reportedly hit; mass casualties alleged
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: AP cites Iranian FM claim and imagery of graves; Reuters cites Iranian reporting and notes UN human rights office demanded an investigation.

incidents.incident_title: Iran Proxy Network Activates: Iraq + Lebanon + Yemen
incidents.summary: Iran activated full proxy network simultaneously: Iraqi PMF; Lebanese Hezbollah; Yemeni Houthi declared readiness to respond to US-Israeli strikes. Three-front simultaneous activation — Iran's asymmetric deterrent answer to losing conventional military exchange.
incidents.assessment: Iran's proxy network is its primary insurance policy — a distributed asymmetric threat that survived the killing of Khamenei and destruction of the IRGC. The three-front activation represents Iran's strategic answer to losing the conventional exchange.
incidents.platform_or_unit: PMF Iraq + Hezbollah Lebanon + Houthi Yemen (all Iran-backed)
incidents.munitions_weapons: Mixed — rockets; missiles; drones; naval mines
incidents.target_type: US bases / Israeli cities / shipping lanes
incidents.result_outcome: 3-front proxy activation — US/Israeli forces threatened across region
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Iraqi PMF threatens US bases in Iraq/Kuwait/Jordan; Hezbollah poses mass rocket threat to Israeli cities; Houthi activates Bab el-Mandeb
incidents.tags: 3-FRONT PROXY; PMF IRAQ; HEZBOLLAH; HOUTHI; ASYMMETRIC

incidents.summary: Iran says its military's chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi has been killed

incidents.incident_title: Iranian Barrage: UAE & Saudi Arabia — 708 Intercepted
incidents.summary: Largest Iranian retaliatory strike: 165 ballistic missiles + 2 cruise missiles + 541 drones fired at UAE and Saudi Arabia. GCC air defenses (Patriot PAC-3; THAAD; Arrow-3; Iron Dome; Tor M1) claimed full intercept. Largest air defense battle in recorded history.
incidents.assessment: 708 munitions in a single engagement tests layered air defense with overwhelming volume — a tactic refined watching Russian drone warfare in Ukraine. The cost-exchange problem: Shahed-136 ~$20k vs Patriot PAC-3 ~$4M — attacker has strong economic advantage.
incidents.munitions_weapons: Ballistic missiles (Shahab/Qiam type); cruise missiles; Shahed-type drones
incidents.target_type: Critical infrastructure — UAE and Saudi Arabia
incidents.result_outcome: All munitions intercepted (claimed)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Largest air defense engagement in history; Shahed-136 costs ~$20000; Patriot PAC-3 costs ~$4M — cost-exchange asymmetry favors attacker
incidents.tags: 165 BALLISTIC MISSILES; 541 DRONES; ALL INTERCEPTED; LARGEST EVER

incidents.incident_title: Opening Maritime Fires: U.S. Navy Tomahawk Strikes on Southern Flank
incidents.origin_sublabel: Launch platforms not named; described as 'shooters at sea'
incidents.target_sublabel: Naval forces / anti-ship missile sites / coastal infrastructure (reported target categories)
incidents.summary: USNI reported Gen. Dan Caine said the first 'shooters at sea' were U.S. Navy Tomahawks that closed on Iranian naval forces and began strikes across Iran’s southern flank; CENTCOM’s first-72-hours fact sheet lists guided-missile destroyers among U.S. assets and includes Iranian navy ships/submarines and anti-ship missile sites among target types.
incidents.assessment: Confirms maritime-launched TLAM use as part of the strike architecture; precise aimpoints, salvo size, and BDA not publicly detailed in cited material.
incidents.result_outcome: Cruise-missile strike campaign ongoing; details undisclosed
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: USNI also cites CENTCOM claim that Iran’s Gulf of Oman naval presence was denied within ~48 hours (11 ships to zero); treat as claim unless independently corroborated per-vessel.

incidents.incident_title: Bandar Abbas: IRIS Shahid Bagheri Drone-Carrier Hit (CENTCOM Acknowledged)
incidents.origin_sublabel: CENTCOM acknowledged strike; exact weapon not specified
incidents.summary: USNI and Aviation Week reported CENTCOM said U.S. forces struck the Iranian drone carrier IRIS Shahid Bagheri within hours of launching Operation Epic Fury; CENTCOM framed it as rebuttal to Iranian claims about a U.S. carrier being hit.
incidents.assessment: High-profile maritime target: a converted container ship configured for UAV operations; public reporting does not specify whether it was struck at pier vs anchorage or the munition used.
incidents.result_outcome: Drone carrier hit (CENTCOM)
incidents.economic_impact: Contributes to broader Hormuz shipping shock and risk escalation in early war days (regional shipping paralysis and anchoring reported).
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Aviation Week cites approximate dimensions and runway length; USNI notes it was attacked at berthing in Bandar Abbas within initial hours per CENTCOM post.

incidents.incident_title: Bandar Abbas: IRIS Makran Forward-Base Ship Struck at Pier (Satellite BDA)
incidents.target_sublabel: IRIS Makran (441) forward-basing ship (converted tanker; large flight deck)
incidents.summary: USNI reported satellite photos show IRIS Makran struck at the pier at Bandar Abbas; The War Zone also assessed Makran was clearly struck at a berth based on Planet Labs imagery comparisons.
incidents.assessment: Makran is a key enabling asset for sustained operations (forward base / aviation support); strike suggests emphasis on degrading Iran’s ability to project and sustain maritime operations.
incidents.result_outcome: Makran struck/damaged (satellite-assessed)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: TWZ notes smoke and imagery limitations can obscure full vessel BDA; USNI emphasizes multiple fires/sunken vessels across Bandar Abbas.

incidents.incident_title: Bandar Abbas: IRIS Kurdistan Hit; Alvand-class Frigate Reported Burning
incidents.origin_sublabel: Reported via satellite imagery / open-source assessment
incidents.target_sublabel: IRIS Kurdistan (floating base) + nearby warship fire (reported)
incidents.summary: CTP-ISW reported satellite imagery showing a strike on the Artesh Navy IRIS Kurdistan in Bandar Abbas port and also reported an unspecified Alvand-class frigate on fire; ABC News Verify reported smoke at Bandar Abbas appears to come from a ship consistent with IRIS Kurdistan, citing ISW assessment.
incidents.assessment: Indicates strikes expanded from combatants to logistics/afloat-basing assets; attribution and munition specifics remain undisclosed in cited reporting.
incidents.result_outcome: IRIS Kurdistan struck (reported); at least one warship burning (reported)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: ABC notes U.S. claims of sinking 11 Iranian ships in the Gulf of Oman since war began (claim); CTP-ISW additionally reports smoke plume at IRGCN 3rd Naval District in Bandar Mahshahr (separate).

incidents.incident_title: Bandar Mahshahr: Smoke Plume at IRGC Navy 3rd Naval District (Reported)
incidents.origin_sublabel: Geolocated imagery referenced by CTP-ISW; limited independent confirmation
incidents.summary: CTP-ISW reported geolocated imagery showing a smoke plume at the IRGC Navy 3rd Naval District in Bandar Mahshahr, Khuzestan Province.
incidents.assessment: Single-source (CTP-ISW) reporting in this pull; without independent imagery confirmation here, treat as possible strike/incident pending corroboration.
incidents.result_outcome: Possible strike/fire (reported)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Bandar Mahshahr is on the northern Persian Gulf littoral; CTP-ISW frames these actions as aimed at limiting retaliatory attacks on shipping and U.S. Navy vessels.

incidents.incident_title: Natanz: Access Points to Underground Enrichment Halls Struck
incidents.target_sublabel: Access points to underground enrichment halls (reported)
incidents.summary: Reuters reported satellite imagery showing strikes on access points at Natanz; an institute assessment said three buildings were destroyed, including two personnel entrance buildings and a vehicle access ramp cover.
incidents.assessment: Physical damage to access infrastructure appears corroborated by commercial satellite imagery; attribution (U.S. vs Israel) is not established in the cited reporting.
incidents.target_depth_hardening: Underground halls; access-point structures above/at entrances
incidents.result_outcome: Access buildings reportedly destroyed; facility access impeded
incidents.nuclear_setback_assessment: Potential setback to access/recovery at Natanz: Reuters cited an assessment that entrances and the only vehicle access ramp cover were destroyed; may affect recoverable centrifuges/equipment.
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters cited the Institute for Science and International Security (David Albright) as assessing two strikes on access points; AP reported Iran’s envoy to IAEA said Natanz was targeted on Sunday; Reuters satellite captions show new building damage at Natanz on March 2.
incidents.think_tank_sources: Institute for Science and International Security (referenced via Reuters)

incidents.incident_title: Choqa Balk-e Alireza: Drone Base Struck
incidents.target_label: Choqa Balk-e Alireza (west of Kermanshah, Iran)
incidents.summary: Reuters satellite imagery captions described destroyed buildings and equipment at the Choqa Balk-e Alireza drone base following a strike on March 2.
incidents.assessment: Satellite captions indicate significant damage; public reporting does not specify which drone units were based there or the exact strike package.
incidents.result_outcome: Buildings/equipment destroyed (reported)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: CENTCOM press release lists 'missile and drone launch sites' among target categories for Operation Epic Fury; Reuters satellite captions document damage at the named drone base.

incidents.incident_title: Bandar Abbas Harbor: Vessel Hit in Airstrike
incidents.target_sublabel: Vessel hit in harbor (per satellite caption)
incidents.summary: Reuters satellite imagery captions described a vessel hit in an airstrike at Bandar Abbas harbor on March 2.
incidents.assessment: Physical hit on a vessel at the harbor is supported by satellite captioning; attribution and vessel identity are not provided in the cited sources.
incidents.result_outcome: Vessel hit; damage extent unclear
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters satellite caption notes a vessel hit; broader reporting indicates U.S./Israel targeted Iranian naval assets during the campaign.

incidents.incident_title: IDF Offensive Campaign: Hezbollah Enters War (Op. Roaring Lion Northern Front)
incidents.origin_sublabel: Ramat David + multiple IAF bases; northern ground forces
incidents.target_sublabel: Hezbollah military infrastructure, C2, communications, Radwan Force
incidents.summary: Hezbollah broke the November 2024 ceasefire on March 1–2, 2026, firing missiles and drones at Israeli bases — Ramat David airbase, Camp Yitzhak, and the Meron monitoring base — declaring it was 'revenge for the blood of Supreme Leader Khamenei.' It was the first Hezbollah cross-border attack since the 2024 ceasefire. Israel responded with an immediate IDF offensive campaign across Lebanon. Within 48 hours: dozens of strikes hit Dahiyeh (south Beirut Hezbollah stronghold), southern Lebanon, and the Beqaa Valley. Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled was killed overnight Mar 2. Al-Manar TV headquarters and al-Nour radio station were struck. Israel issued evacuation orders to 50+ villages and 59 areas including Dahiyeh neighborhoods. IDF ground forces pushed into southern Lebanon in a new incursion on Mar 3, seizing 'strategic areas' and commanding high ground. Lebanon's army pulled back from at least 7 border positions. 30,000+ displaced. IDF Chief of Staff Zamir declared: 'We have begun an offensive campaign against Hezbollah. We are not just on defense, now we go on the offense.' Netanyahu authorized IDF to 'advance and take control of additional commanding areas in Lebanon.' Israel is calling up tens of thousands of reservists near the Lebanese border, fueling speculation of deeper ground invasion.
incidents.assessment: Hezbollah entered the war significantly weakened from the 2024 conflict — IDF had already eliminated 267+ operatives during the ceasefire period. Naim Qassem declined to join the June 2025 12-day war; his decision to enter in March 2026 reflects Iranian pressure post-Khamenei assassination. The IDF ground incursion follows a familiar pattern: use air strikes to suppress air defense and communications, then advance ground forces to buffer zone positions. Lebanon's government banning Hezbollah's military activities signals political fracture within Lebanon but Hezbollah dismissed it. The dual threat to Israel (Iran campaign + Lebanon front) is exactly the multi-front scenario the IDF had been planning for under Op. Roaring Lion. IDF COS Zamir confirmed: 'The IDF planned and is prepared to operate in multiple arenas simultaneously.'
incidents.munitions_weapons: F-35I Adir; F-16I Sufa; F-15I Ra'am; air-launched PGMs; JDAM; SDB; artillery
incidents.target_type: Hezbollah military HQ / C2 / communications / missile infrastructure / Radwan Force / intelligence apparatus
incidents.target_depth_hardening: Surface — urban and semi-urban; reinforced Dahiyeh infrastructure
incidents.military_kia_enemy: Hezbollah intel chief Hussein Makled (confirmed KIA); senior Radwan Force operatives
incidents.civilian_kia: At least 52 killed, 154 injured in Lebanese strikes (state media, Mar 2)
incidents.personnel_deployed: Tens of thousands of reservists being called up
incidents.result_outcome: IDF offensive campaign ongoing; ground incursion in progress; Hezbollah ceasefire broken; 30,000+ displaced in Lebanon
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Hezbollah broke Nov 2024 US-brokered ceasefire; Lebanon Army withdrew from 7+ border posts; Lebanese govt banned Hezbollah military activity (cabinet vote); Israel previously maintained 5 border posts in Lebanon citing Hezbollah non-disarmament; IDF pre-planned multi-front operation
incidents.osint_sources: Alma Research Center (monthly IDF Lebanon strike tracker Feb 2026); UNIFIL reports
incidents.press_sources: Times of Israel; Al Jazeera; CNN; Axios; AFP; AP

incidents.incident_title: Targeted Strike: Hezbollah Intelligence Chief Hussein Makled Killed
incidents.origin_sublabel: Precision airstrike on senior Hezbollah leadership
incidents.target_label: Beirut area — Hezbollah intelligence HQ location
incidents.summary: Overnight March 2–3, 2026, Israel conducted a precision strike in the Beirut area killing Hussein Makled, identified by the IDF as the head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters. The IDF stated Makled was responsible for 'forming the intelligence picture using various intelligence collection tools to provide the Hezbollah terror organization with intelligence assessments regarding IDF troops and the State of Israel.' Simultaneously, the IDF struck a key Radwan Force operative in southern Lebanon. Defense Minister Katz publicly declared Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem 'a marked target for elimination.' Reports also indicated strikes were targeting Naim Qassem himself and Mohammad Raad (head of Hezbollah's parliamentary bloc) — Raad's whereabouts were unknown and his body was being searched for in rubble per Al Hadath.
incidents.assessment: Makled's elimination degrades Hezbollah's real-time intelligence on IDF movements — critical as Israel launches its ground incursion into southern Lebanon. Targeting the intelligence chief in the opening hours of an offensive is a deliberate pattern (see: 12-Day War opening salvo eliminating 40 IRGC commanders). Katz's public designation of Qassem as a 'marked target' signals an impending leadership decapitation attempt, following the same Roaring Lion doctrine applied to Khamenei. The simultaneous pursuit of Raad (political-military figure) suggests Israel is pursuing both military and political-military Hezbollah leadership simultaneously.
incidents.military_kia_enemy: Hussein Makled (Hezbollah intelligence chief, confirmed KIA); Radwan Force operative in south Lebanon (KIA)
incidents.civilian_kia: Civilians in vicinity (unknown — not reported separately)
incidents.result_outcome: Hezbollah intelligence chief killed; Radwan Force operative killed; Qassem designated 'marked target'; Raad status unknown
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: IDF pre-identified Makled as intelligence architecture lead; strike conducted within hours of Hezbollah entering war — indicates pre-planned target list was immediately activated upon Hezbollah fire
incidents.osint_sources: Times of Israel (confirmed IDF statement); Al Hadath (Raad body search)
incidents.press_sources: Times of Israel; IDF official statement; Axios

incidents.incident_title: Hormuz Crisis: -70% Tanker Traffic Collapse
incidents.summary: Iran threatened Strait of Hormuz via IRGCN fast-boat harassment; mine-laying; anti-ship missile threats. Tanker traffic collapsed 70%. 150+ ships anchored outside strait. Brent crude +9% to $79.45 (intraday +12-13%). War-risk insurance +50%. Qatar LNG facilities struck; production halted.
incidents.assessment: Worst global energy disruption since 1973 Arab oil embargo. Unlike prior Hormuz threats, active war context made the threat credible enough to collapse transits. Fertilizer impact creates downstream food price ripple 6–12 months later.
incidents.platform_or_unit: IRGCN fast boats + mine-laying assets + anti-ship missiles
incidents.result_outcome: De-facto blockade — tanker traffic -70%
incidents.economic_impact: -70% tanker traffic; 150+ ships anchored outside Hormuz; Brent crude +9% → $79.45; intraday +12–13%; war-risk insurance +50%; Qatar LNG halted
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: 20% global petroleum transits Hormuz; 33% global fertilizer trade transits Hormuz; Qatar LNG hit halts key European alternative supply
incidents.tags: -70% TRAFFIC; 20% GLOBAL OIL; $79.45 BRENT; QATAR LNG HIT

incidents.incident_title: Qom: Assembly of Experts Building Flattened (Reported)
incidents.target_sublabel: Building housing Assembly of Experts (reported by Iranian news agencies)
incidents.summary: Reuters reported Iranian news agencies said an air attack in Qom flattened the building housing Iran's Assembly of Experts, the body tasked with selecting a new supreme leader. An AP report also noted strikes reported in Qom against a building linked to the clerical panel, and said Iranian media reported it was empty at the time.
incidents.assessment: Target description is based on Iranian media reports cited by Reuters/AP; on-the-ground independent confirmation and casualty details were unclear in the cited coverage.
incidents.result_outcome: Building flattened (reported); casualties unclear
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters: Iranian news agencies described the Assembly of Experts building as flattened; AP-affiliate report: strikes in Qom reported, building said empty.

incidents.incident_title: Tehran: IRIB State Broadcaster & Mehrabad Airport Area Hit
incidents.summary: Reuters reported Israel struck the state broadcaster IRIB and an area around Tehran’s Mehrabad airport amid repeated explosions in the capital.
incidents.assessment: Reuters provides the core description; additional independent detail on damage/casualties in the cited window is limited.
incidents.result_outcome: Areas struck; damage extent unclear
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: This strike reporting aligns with broader descriptions of sustained attacks in Tehran on March 3.

incidents.incident_title: Tehran: Minzadehei Underground Nuclear Development Site Hit (Reported)
incidents.summary: Reuters reported the Israeli military said it hit the Minzadehei underground nuclear development site in Tehran.
incidents.assessment: This is a claim attributed to the Israeli military in Reuters reporting; independent imagery or official corroboration is not included in the cited sources here.
incidents.result_outcome: Hit claimed; damage not independently confirmed in cited sources
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters notes the claim as part of broader Tehran strikes on March 3.

incidents.incident_title: Tehran: Presidential Complex Domed Roof Destroyed (Satellite Imagery)
incidents.summary: AP reported satellite images from Colorado-based company Vantor showed the domed roof of Iran’s presidential complex in Tehran had been destroyed, supporting Israel’s claim of an overnight strike; Iran did not acknowledge damage or casualties in that report.
incidents.assessment: Damage is supported by commercial satellite imagery cited by AP; attribution and casualty reporting are uncertain in the cited coverage.
incidents.result_outcome: Structural damage to domed roof (reported)
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: The cited AP report states Iran did not acknowledge the damage or report casualties; imagery credited to Vantor.

incidents.incident_title: Indian Ocean: U.S. Submarine Torpedoes IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka
incidents.target_sublabel: International waters (reported); ship identified as IRIS Dena by Sri Lankan official in Reuters reporting
incidents.summary: Reuters and AP reporting described a U.S. submarine torpedo sinking an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka, with Reuters identifying the frigate as IRIS Dena and reporting casualties/rescues; AP reported the Defense Secretary said it was the first such torpedo sinking since WWII and that Sri Lankan authorities rescued survivors.
incidents.assessment: This is a major escalation in naval operations beyond Iranian home waters; reported high casualties and demonstrates U.S. willingness to strike Iranian naval units far from the Gulf.
incidents.result_outcome: Warship sunk
incidents.economic_impact: Shipping paralysis around Hormuz was reported to persist for a fifth day as this strike occurred (broader naval/economic context).
incidents.key_intelligence_notes: Reuters reports: 32 rescued; 87 bodies recovered; ~60 unaccounted from ~180 crew; Sri Lankan official said vessel heading back from eastern India.

imagery.label: B-2 Spirit Stealth Bomber
imagery.caption: B-2 Spirit dropping ordnance. Seven flew round-trip from Diego Garcia (~13,000 km) requiring 52 tankers.

imagery.label: GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator
imagery.caption: GBU-57A/B MOP — 30,000 lb, Eglin steel alloy casing. Fordow required 12 (vs 2 at Natanz).

imagery.label: Natanz — Satellite Pre-Strike
imagery.caption: Pre-strike satellite imagery of Iranian nuclear enrichment infrastructure. Natanz houses centrifuge halls ~8m underground with reinforced concrete.

imagery.label: F-35I Adir — Primary Strike Platform
imagery.caption: F-35I Adir — Israeli customized variant with indigenous avionics. Low-observable design penetrated Iran's S-300 network.
imagery.source: Israeli Air Force / Wikimedia Commons (public domain)

imagery.label: S-300 Battery — Neutralized
imagery.caption: S-300PMU2 batteries could not track the F-35I at combat altitude — rendered effectively blind by stealth.

imagery.label: ESA Sentinel-2 — Commercial Satellite (Detection Method)
imagery.caption: Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery (10m resolution). OSINT analysts cross-referenced AIS gaps with satellite data to confirm the vessel's classified mission profile days before the operation launched.

imagery.label: USS Lake Erie (CG-70) — Ticonderoga-class Cruiser Escort
imagery.caption: USS Lake Erie (CG-70) assigned as protective escort for MV Ocean Trader.

imagery.label: MH-60 SOF Helicopter Insertion
imagery.caption: MH-60 Black Hawk variant used for SOF insertion from the Ocean Trader to the Caracas target.

imagery.label: SOF Helicopter Raid — Caracas Insertion
imagery.caption: SOF forces inserted by helicopter from the MV Ocean Trader staging platform. Zero US casualties.

imagery.label: Tomahawk VLS Launch — Arleigh Burke DDG
imagery.caption: Tomahawk Block IV launch from Vertical Launch System. 13 Arleigh Burke DDGs contributed to the 900+ strike opening salvo.

imagery.label: HIMARS — PrSM Launch Platform
imagery.caption: US Army HIMARS firing from Kuwait. PrSM made its combat debut from these platforms, demonstrating 499+ km range.

imagery.label: JDAM Bunker Strike Munition
imagery.caption: GBU-31 Joint Direct Attack Munition loaded in a B-2 Spirit — precision guidance kit for bunker targeting. CIA penetration enabled location of Khamenei's underground bunker.

imagery.label: Shahed-136 Kamikaze Drone
imagery.caption: Shahed-136 loitering munition — one of 541 drones in the 708-munition barrage. ~$20,000 each vs. $4M for a Patriot PAC-3 interceptor.

imagery.label: Patriot PAC-3 Intercept
imagery.caption: Patriot PAC-3 missile intercept. The layered GCC + US defense architecture intercepted all 708 munitions.

imagery.label: PLAN Shandong (CV-17) — Type 001A Carrier
imagery.caption: PLA Navy carrier in South China Sea operations.

imagery.label: Fiery Cross Reef — PLAN Artificial Island Base
imagery.caption: Fiery Cross Reef artificial island — 3,000m runway, hardened aircraft shelters, SAM/CDCM batteries.
imagery.source: CSIS AMTI / Planet Labs satellite imagery

imagery.label: China Coast Guard (CCG) Vessel
imagery.caption: China Coast Guard vessel — primary gray-zone coercion tool.

imagery.label: BRP Sierra Madre — Philippine Military Outpost
imagery.caption: BRP Sierra Madre (AT-43) — a deliberately grounded WWII-era LST serving as the Philippines' military outpost on Second Thomas Shoal.

imagery.label: USS Arleigh Burke DDG — FONOP Platform
imagery.caption: Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer — the primary US Navy platform for SCS FONOP operations.

imagery.label: Mischief Reef — CSIS AMTI Satellite (2025)
imagery.caption: PLA artificial island base — Mischief Reef, 250km from Palawan, Philippine EEZ.
imagery.source: CSIS AMTI / Planet Labs (public satellite imagery)

imagery.label: H-6K Nuclear-Capable Bomber — PLAAF
imagery.caption: PLAAF H-6K Xian bomber — nuclear-capable platform in the 103-aircraft ADIZ incursion.
